target.name: Built-in ext init plugin scaffold and first-party templates

reviewer.relationship: First-party self-review of the built-in scaffold

domains.name: security
domains.notes: The CLI validates template identity and rejects unknown or conflicting options before filesystem mutation without reflecting attacker-controlled option values. All three templates request no capabilities, deny egress, have no side effects, and reject undeclared hooks. Invoice amounts must be non-negative safe integers. The API policy rejects malformed methods and ambiguous paths, enforces a path-segment boundary, and returns fixed deny reasons without payload reflection. Every generated bundle is audited before its generated tests run.

domains.name: compatibility
domains.notes: The generic init contract remains covered, while typed webhook, invoice, and API policy templates fix their reviewed hooks and types. Generated manifest and package metadata use the CLI version exactly, then all three templates build and test against packed public package contents.

domains.name: operations
domains.notes: Generated CPU and timeout limits are bounded, network access is denied, and template resolution is local and deterministic. Invoice and API policies use pure bounded checks with fail-closed input handling; the accountless E2E exercises install, audit, build, and test boundaries for all three templates.

domains.name: documentation
domains.notes: The authoring quickstart and CLI reference document template generation, option conflicts, verification commands, registry blocker, and security boundary. Every template scaffold emits a SECURITY.md, and the human checklist states all non-guarantees.

domains.name: license
domains.notes: The repository and CLI declare Apache-2.0, and the scaffold generates source-only first-party content without vendored third-party assets.

unverified.item: Install generated dependencies from the public npm registry
unverified.reason: The TenantScript npm scope is not yet activated; the E2E uses freshly packed public package contents instead.
This record is an explicitly first-party review of the repository-owned baseline.

limitations: The decision covers only the listed scaffold source digests and their reachable baseline context.
The scaffold is intentionally minimal and does not prove the safety of code added by a plugin author.
The webhook transformer demonstrates a side-effect-free payload pass-through; adopters must add schema validation and output bounds for their concrete business contract.
The invoice auto-approval threshold is an example, not evidence of trusted actor identity or a production accounting, legal, or customer-specific approval rule.
The API policy expects a host-authenticated caller and a decoded, normalized pathname; it is not an authentication, tenant RBAC, URL canonicalization, or complete authorization policy.
A finding-free bundle audit does not prove the absence of aliased, dynamic, templated, dead-code, or runtime-only behavior.
nonGuarantees: This approval is not a security certification or an independent audit.
It does not claim live deployment, public registry installation, or compatibility with later commits and dependency resolutions.
